title: Director, Transactions Management
topic_label: property management services, property management, chambers property management
description: About the role You will deliver Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management (TM) services to some of the world’s largest companies. This position calls for an energetic, detail-oriented professional with excellent project management, analytical, communication and collaboration skills as well as experience of managing real estate strategies and transactions across Asia Pacific. Comfortable in working towards achieving performance targets, you will be responsible to deliver a range of possible real estate and workplace solutions to clients looking for the next generation benefits that come from a vested partnership with JLL. Portfolio Strategy & Planning – Country, City and Metro plans, Global Initiatives Assessment of portfolio performance, portfolio rationalization and divestment, lease vs. ownership structures, cost reduction initiatives and bid support to achieve business change. Lead planning process to develop strategies to lower real estate costs and improve workplace effectiveness. Identify opportunities for optimization, cost saving and improved ROI in the Client portfolio Conduct fact-based analysis using occupancy, portfolio, real estate, and business data to align stakeholders on informed real estate plans. Through strong business relationships and clear, compelling business cases, challenge status quo thinking to drive unexpected portfolio optimization opportunities Develop overarching strategies related to workplace, organization, process, sustainability, operations and finance to govern the portfolio and set a foundation for city master plans Analyze inputs from the strategic planning process and on-account systems (business requirements, costs, facility information, cap-ex requirements, forecasts, occupancy data, lease and market data) Analyze and make recommendations with respect to strategic dispositions, investment and market opportunities, hold versus sell analysis, feasibility studies, asset review and market studies, highest and best use analysis, acquisition and advice analysis and valuations for sale/leaseback purposes Develop multiple practical options supported by strong business cases that optimize NPV and enhance productivity and innovation Be a SPOC for all Portfolio related client inquiries and a representative in global calls and initiatives Manage expectations, timelines and delivery against Client requirements, represent both JLL and the Client’s interest on the long term Transaction Management You will execute a wide variety of real estate transactions, including capital and lease acquisitions, sale and leasebacks and disposition transactions through local Markets teams across Asia, demonstrating a high level of proficiency and knowledge. You will actively engage with client’s senior stakeholders to define objectives and agree requirements at the outset of a strategy and transaction. Financial Acumen You will need to: Prepare and evaluate financial analysis documents. Support and contribute to the client’s budget forecasting. Forecast revenue and initiate and close-out projects to meet monthly cashflow plan. Monitor and closely control legal budgets and resolve billing queries. Relationship Management You will build strong and sustainable working relationships and help the client navigate through the JLL delivery platform in an efficient and productive manner. You will be the face of Portfolio Strategy and Transaction Management towards the Client both regionally and globally. People Management You possess strong leadership skills to manage brokers across geographies and actively engage across the client’s wider corporate real estate team and internal stakeholders in a collaborative manner. You demonstrate a high level of proficiency and knowledge in portfolio management and possesses the ability to orchestrate complex activities across multiple countries/properties/services at once to accomplish goals and objectives. Commercial Acumen You will understand account profitability, formulate an account plan to achieve revenue and performance targets whilst meeting client service standards. Armed with a sharp business acumen, you will leverage on technology and new products within JLL to deliver the next generation of benefits for our client. Operating a Global Account Can you see the bigger picture? Can you juggle regional delivery that aligns to a global strategy? As the TM lead for Asia on a Global Account, you are proactive in bringing Asia into the forefront, highlighting successes or potential issues whilst providing tangible solutions for the client. Can you communicate clearly and guide the client and your team on the best way forward for best in class processes and portfolio strategies at an account level? Sound like you? Here is what we’re looking for: Great Communicator Can you get your ideas across clearly both verbally and in writing? You’ll be working with many diverse interest groups so the way you share information and your ability to persuasively convey information is highly important. A high EQ is must for this role. You will have exceptional client management, communication and presentation skills and the ability to absorb and translate broad concepts and ideas into clear and logical communications. Ambitious You will be results-oriented with confidence to liaise effectively with all levels of management. Have commitment to collaboration and teamwork, and would thrive to exceed team goals. Resilient Transaction Management is a highly dynamic part of JLL’s business. It’s fast paced and you’ll need to be fast paced as well. You’ll need a positive mind set and a ‘can do’ attitude when multi-tasking driving change and delivering efficiencies. Naturally proactive, you’ll possess the innate ability to identify risks in delivery before it becomes an issue. At JLL, we have a continuous improvement mentality across our business and we’ll be looking for you to have the same. A flexible, resourceful and proactive individual who is able to produce results and guide the team to deliver the same. You will have the ability to work well under pressure and support the team without compromising quality. Experienced You will be an experienced real estate professional with a minimum of 10-12 years’ experience who has delivered positive results across the APAC region. You will be familiar with working with fast moving MNC clients with a regional presence and multiple service lines and possess prior experience in managing a team.

title: Senior Finance Analyst
topic_label: property management services, property management, chambers property management
description: Key Responsibilities 1. Financial Modelling & Investment Analysis Build and maintain development and acquisition financial models (DCF, IRR, NPV, equity waterfall, sensitivity analysis). Analyse GDV, construction costs, land cost, financing costs, exit values and cash-flow timing. Evaluate different ownership and investment structures (direct holding, SPV, JV, fund structures). Stress-test assumptions under various market, interest-rate and sales-velocity scenarios. 2. Singapore Property Market Analysis Conduct micro-market research by district, sub-market, and asset class (residential, industrial, commercial). Track URA data, transaction trends, price indices, rental yields, take-up rates, and supply pipelines. Assess the impact of government policies (ABSD, BSD, SSD, LTV limits, cooling measures, GLS/IGLS supply). Monitor URA Master Plan and infrastructure developments affecting asset values. 3. Deal Evaluation & Due Diligence Support feasibility studies for new developments, redevelopment and value-add opportunities. Review key due-diligence inputs: valuation reports, cost plans, sales assumptions and consultants’ reports. Assess project risks (planning, construction, sales, financing, regulatory) and propose mitigation strategies. 4. Investment Committee & Stakeholder Support Prepare investment memoranda, IC papers, and executive summaries for senior management and investors. Translate complex financial outputs into clear insights and recommendations. 5. Portfolio & Performance Monitoring Track actual vs forecast performance for live projects and stabilised assets. Analyse deviations in costs, sales velocity, pricing and cash flows. Support periodic asset revaluation and portfolio reporting. Requirements Technical & Analytical Strong proficiency in Excel financial modelling (advanced formulas, scenario analysis). Solid understanding of real estate finance metrics: IRR, NPV, DSCR, LTV, ICR, GDV. Familiarity with Singapore property regulations and tax framework. Ability to interpret market data and translate it into investment insights. Professional Background Degree in Finance, Real Estate, Economics, Accounting, or related discipline. 2–5 years of experience in real estate investment, development, advisory, valuation, or fund management. Experience covering Singapore property is strongly preferred. Soft Skills High attention to detail with strong commercial judgement. Clear written and verbal communication skills (able to brief senior stakeholders). Ability to work across multiple projects and tight timelines. Strong ethics and professionalism in handling confidential information.

description: PropertyGuru is Southeast Asia’s leading PropTech company, and the preferred destination for over 32 million property seekers monthly to connect with over 50,000 agents monthly to find their dream home. About the Role As a Key Account Manager , you will play a pivotal role in growing PropertyGuru’s business by developing strategic client relationships, uncovering new opportunities, and driving value for some of our most important partners. You will combine deep market understanding with strong commercial acumen to deliver solutions that help our clients win and fuel PropertyGuru’s continued leadership in the region. As a Guru you will be accountable for: Drive strategic account growth by identifying new and existing business opportunities and expanding PropertyGuru’s presence within key client segments. Build and nurture long-term partnerships , ensuring clients receive consistent value and an exceptional service experience. Conduct detailed market and competitor analysis to identify trends, opportunities, and emerging client needs. Drive sales initiatives and conduct trainings to increase product adoption, drive revenue growth, and exceed sales targets. Develop compelling proposals and presentations that clearly communicate our value propositions and data-driven insights. Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Marketing, Operations, to align on solutions with broader business objectives and client requirements. Support and work alongside with senior key account manager to achieve team targets We're looking for someone who: Proven experience in business development or key account management , with a track record of meeting or exceeding sales goals. Hands-on experience with cold calling and face-to-face client meetings , with the confidence and communication skills to engage prospects, pitch value propositions, and convert opportunities into revenue. You are comfortable initiating conversations, handling objections, and building rapport quickly in both virtual and in-person settings Strong strategic and commercial thinking , with the ability to identify opportunities and execute effective growth plans. Exceptional relationship management skills , including the ability to influence, consult, and build trust with clients. Analytical and data-driven mindset , capable of interpreting market insights to guide recommendations and decision-making. Highly proactive, self-motivated, and outcome-oriented , with strong leadership qualities and a passion for driving client success. Strong team player , you actively collaborate, support teammates, and contribute to a positive, high-performance team culture. Ability to work effectively with minimal supervision , demonstrating maturity, accountability, and strong organizational skills. You can prioritize tasks, manage time independently, and consistently deliver results in a fast-paced environment. Our commitment to you: Hybrid flexible working that focuses on outcomes over hours. Holistic rewards package covering your financial, physical & mental health. Multi-directional career development across all levels. Inclusive benefits like equal paternity leave, supporting all employees in work-life balance.

title: Industrial Property Consultant
topic_label: property management services, property management, chambers property management
description: About the Role We are seeking a motivated and analytical individual to join our consultancy team as an Industrial Property Consultant . In this role, you will work closely with industrial clients to develop a thorough understanding of their business operations, financial position, workforce profile and operational requirements. This understanding will be applied to support property-related decisions that have direct implications on the client’s operations, cost structure and long-term planning. The role is primarily analytical and report-driven. This position is well suited to individuals who are comfortable working with data, structured documentation requirements and who enjoy producing clear and substantiated reports. Key Responsibilities Business and Operational Assessment • Review clients’ financial statements. • Analyze clients’ business models and operational workflows. • Review employee headcount, manpower composition and organizational structure. • Assess operational scale, space utilization and site requirements. Property Planning Support and Reporting • Prepare structured analyses for industrial property planning. • Draft reports for property-related applications. • Develop structured strategies relating to space requirements and site usage. • Ensure reports and submissions are accurate, consistent and complete. Coordination and Engagement • Obtain information and data from clients. • Clarify data and assumptions with relevant parties. • Respond to queries on reports and supporting materials. • Coordinate documentation with internal and external parties. • Manage multiple assignments and timelines concurrently. Qualifications and Requirements • Ability to review and interpret financial statements. • Strong analytical capability. • Strong written communication and report-writing skills. • Proficiency in Microsoft Word and Excel. • Organized and detail-oriented. • Ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines. • Diploma or higher qualification in any discipline. • Prior experience in business analysis, reporting, consultancy support or property-related roles is an advantage. What We Offer • Competitive monthly remuneration. • Performance-based incentives. • Annual performance bonus and Annual Wage Supplement. • On-the-job training and guidance. • Professional and collaborative working environment.

title: Assistant Manager/Manager - Portfolio & Asset Management (Real Estate)
topic_label: property management services, property management, chambers property management
description: This is a newly created position and working very closely with an established senior management team that embrace an excellent work culture of performance. They are a multi-asset firm with real estate as one of their biggest portfolios. With their value-add strategies, you will be looking to be very hands-on in managing their asset enhancement initiatives. Their portfolio will comprise of logistics, living and healthcare. You will be responsible for a wide scope of work including portfolio management, asset management, lease, project and development management. This will include formulating and executing overall fund strategy, including overseeing asset allocation and asset management plans. You will oversee, prepare and manage annual asset plan / budgets and financial reports, with the assistance of the asset management and finance teams. You will recommend asset enhancement, redevelopment, divestment opportunities and managing the sale process. You will participate in any potential development/ investment transaction process. You will be managing and work closely with the appointed property agents to ensure the assets are well-managed. You will liaise with various stakeholders in the conceptualization, formulation and implementation of asset enhancement. You will be supporting the investment team in property acquisitions, fund raising, fund management, general corporate finance work too. You will be responsible for carrying out financial modelling and assist in feasibility studies to support projects identified for major real estate asset enhancements, redevelopment and transformation. You will assist in analysing, planning of real estate assets within the portfolio to determine its highest and best use. As part of the Lease Management, you will maintain good tenant relationships and oversee in lease negotiations to ensure high occupancy rate including setting leasing strategy, re-gearing leases and negotiate surrender/re-letting opportunities. You will be responsible for driving the global leasing of properties within the portfolio, including attending to securing tenants. You will be analysing and reviewing rental, renewal options and prepositions as well as negotiation. This will include reviewing and recommending lease terms, negotiating with prospective tenants to achieve desired rent and occupancy. You will also be involved in Project and Development Management such as managing the budget, resources, scheduling etc. You will oversee site entitlement and due diligence effort for new construction and re-modelling projects across portfolio of real estate. You will participate in project scoping and budgeting as well as managing all aspects of the due diligence process including but not limited to feasibility studies, geotechnical evaluations, environmental assessments, and surveys. Requirements: Degree in Finance, Real Estate, or equivalent. Preferably at least 5 years’ experience in similar capacity either Portfolio or Asset Management or Development management Strong financial skills, analytical mind, and creative thinking Excellent verbal and written communication skills Fast learner, ability to work independently, multi-task, and execute efficiently in a fast-paced dynamic environment All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence. Personal data provided will be used for recruitment purposes only. Please apply with your CV in word document format. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. For other positions related to real estate, please go to https://careers.tri-cap.com.sg/. EA Licence: 24S2282 EA Personnel: R1109270

Our highly committed HQ provides business support and technical expertise, while our established procedures and SOPs adhere to market best practices. professional and courteous demeanor in all communications to foster a positive and supportive environment Manage overall office administration to ensure organized and efficient operations Advise the responses Coordinate and engage contractors, solicitors, auditors, architects, engineers, and other professionals with prior Corporation approval to execute maintenance and project works Act on behalf title: Finance / Accounting Trainer (Freelance/Part Time)
description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Delivery of Core Accounting Concepts: Double Entry, Accounting Standards & Accounting Cycle •	Financial Statement Analysis: Guide learners on how to read and interpret the Profit & Loss (Statement of Comprehensive Income) and Balance Sheet (Statement of Financial Position). •	Interpretation of financials: Teach calculation of key financial ratios (Current Ratio, ROI, Gross Margin) to assess business health. •	Data Analytics & Visualization: Guide learners on extracting raw financial data and transforming it into actionable insights. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person, virtual, or hybrid). •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. Administrative Responsibilities: •	Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner. •	Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data. •	Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards. Learner Support and Engagement: •	Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve. •	Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment. •	Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students. Requirements •	ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required. •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. •	Professional certification (e.g., CA, ACCA, CPA) is a plus. •	At least 3–5 years of practical accounting experience. •	Strong presentation, facilitation, and interpersonal skills. •	Prior training or teaching experience is highly desirable. •	Ability to simplify complex financial concepts for diverse learners.

title: AI Finance / Accounting Trainer (Freelance/Part Time)
description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Design and develop AI training content, including both core/beginner and advanced levels. •	Prompt Engineering for Finance: Teach learners how to construct precise prompts to generate financial summaries, draft overdue payment emails, and interpret P&L variances using LLMs (Large Language Models). •	Excel & AI Integration: Guide learners on using AI features within spreadsheets (e.g., Copilot for Excel) to clean data and generate formulas automatically. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person)making complex AI tools accessible and relevant to learners. •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. Administrative Responsibilities: •	Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner. •	Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data. •	Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards. Learner Support and Engagement: •	Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve. •	Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment. •	Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students. Requirements •	ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required. •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. •	Prior experience teaching (preferably 3 years) or training adult learners in AI / digital technology. •	Professional certification (e.g., CA, ACCA, CPA) is a plus. •	At least 3–5 years of practical accounting experience. •	Strong presentation, facilitation, and interpersonal skills. •	Prior training or teaching experience is highly desirable. •	Ability to simplify complex financial concepts for diverse learners.

title: Wealth Strategist

description: Responsibilities: 1. Portfolio Analysis: - Conduct in-depth analysis of investment portfolios and risk factors. - Utilize financial techniques to evaluate potential investment opportunities. 2. Technology Utilization: - Stay current on advancements in financial technology to enhance operational efficiency. 10. Continual Learning: - Engage in ongoing professional development to stay current on industry trends, investment strategies, and financial products. - Pursue relevant certifications to enhance knowledge and credibility in the field.

title: FINANCIAL CONSULTANT (EXPAT WEALTH MARKET)

Arrange suitable insurance for the client, recommending the amount and type of coverage based on a needs analysis Keep abreast of the financial markets, constantly monitoring the specific investments in the client’s portfolios, and keep up to date with new investment strategies and investment vehicles. Develop and cultivate a client base through on going servicing. Attend Financial Advisers meetings and continuing education sessions to retain and develop knowledge about products, services, policies, and acquire the required relevant CPD certification. Prepare and deliver presentations/seminars to clients and prospects for business development purposes. Comply with all industry rules and regulations. Work alongside local Marketing Team to create an individual sales and marketing strategy for cultivating new client relationships. In-depth understanding of the expat wealth management market in Singapore Other duties: Adhere to Company policies and procedures Deliver a Client centric advisory process covering the holistic needs of clients both investment and protection Qualifications required: M5, M8, M8A, M9, M9A, HI (essential) CHFC or CFP accredited (desired) UK Financial Services qualifications (an advantage) Skills and Requirements Self-driven individual who has passion to excel Strong communication and interpersonal skills Minimum 5 years’ experience providing face-to-face financial advice Good knowledge of current market performance and investment strategies Strong Knowledge of Financial Planning/Wealth Management advisory process Strong understanding of UK/European/Australian market knowledge including but not limited to Pension and Tax ideally with a UK relevant financial services qualification Strong client base and a large network of High-Net-Worth individuals, with proven track record of turning prospects into clients and existing funds under management

title: Finance Manager
description: About the Company A leading technology solutions provider delivering scalable, secure, and AI-enabled platforms to clients across the Asia-Pacific region. The company is at the forefront of cloud, SaaS, and IT innovation, driving digital transformation across industries. Role Overview The Finance Manager will play a pivotal role in driving financial consolidation, reporting, and strategic finance initiatives across the group. This role involves leading multi-entity and multi-region consolidation, ensuring compliance with IFRS/GAAP standards, and partnering closely with senior management to deliver actionable insights that inform business strategy. The position also contributes to budgeting, forecasting, and finance transformation initiatives, supporting the company’s growth in a fast-paced tech environment. Key Responsibilities Lead financial consolidation and reporting across multiple entities and regions in compliance with IFRS/GAAP. Manage intercompany eliminations, foreign currency translations, and equity accounting to ensure accuracy and transparency. Collaborate with regional finance teams to standardize reporting processes, ensure timely delivery, and maintain data integrity. Provide financial insights and performance analysis to senior leadership, enabling strategic decision-making and business growth. Drive continuous improvement in financial processes, including consolidation, forecasting, and finance transformation initiatives. Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field; CPA/CA qualification is a plus. Minimum 6 years of relevant experience in financial reporting, consolidation, or audit, ideally within MNCs or tech/SaaS companies. Strong technical knowledge of IFRS or local GAAP; hands-on experience in financial consolidation and reporting. Proficient in Microsoft Office; experience with SAP, NetSuite, or other ERP systems preferred. Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills, with proven stakeholder management capabilities. Reg. No: R21103564 EA License: 23S1921

title: Senior Manager, Finance
description: The Senior Finance Manager of MCST oversees and manages professionally all financial aspects of the property, including budgeting, financial reporting, and ensuring compliance with relevant regulations such as BMSMA and IRAS, while also managing the MCST's finances and advising the financial implications to facilitate decision by the Management, Executive Committee and Council. Key Responsibilities: Financial Planning and Budgeting: Develop and manage the MCST's annual budget, ensuring it aligns with the needs and financial goals. Prepare and present financial reports, including income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements, to the MCST Executive Committee and Council. Conduct financial forecasting and analysis to anticipate future financial needs and risks. Financial Management: Manage the MCST's financial resources, including collecting and disbursing funds, managing bank accounts, and overseeing investments, all within the BMSMA. Monitor and track expenses, ensuring they are within budget and compliant with regulations. Oversee the payment of invoices and other financial obligations. Compliance and Reporting: Ensure compliance with relevant laws and regulations, including the Strata Titles Act and other relevant legislation. Maintain accurate financial records and documentation. Work with auditors to ensure the MCST's financial statements are accurate and compliant. Stakeholder Communication: Communicate financial information to the MCST council, subsidiary proprietors, and other stakeholders in a clear and concise manner. Address financial inquiries and concerns from subsidiary proprietors and other stakeholders. AGM and Key Liaison Person: Overall lead in managing and organizing the MCST AGM. MCST Key holder to all subsidiary proprietors’ confidential records. Main liaison point with external legal counsel on all MCST financial and AGM matters, including required filing of information with IRAS and BMSMA. Liaise with external and internal auditors, tax agent and lawyers to support audit, tax and other statutory requirements. Coordinate with group HQ, internal stakeholders and other relevant departments. Finance Team Lead: Lead a team of finance specialists, provide guidance and support to the team to ensure financial operation is delivered according to guidelines and timeliness. Manage and develop the Finance team through general supervision, training and development, KPI setting and monitoring, one-on-one meetings and coaching etc. Conduct process reviews periodically to identify any inefficiencies, and define areas for improvement, ensuring processes remain relevant, align with MCST’s goals, and foster continuous improvement. Other Duties: Work with other MCST departments and staff to ensure the smooth operation of the workflows, providing guidance on finance topics when necessary. Stay up-to-date with changes in relevant laws and regulations. Any other finance related duties and projects that may be assigned from time-to-time. Key Requirements: Degree in Accountancy or equivalent with at least 15 years of relevant working experience, of which at least 8 years in a managerial position. Good understanding of Singapore financial reporting standards and local tax rules. Knowledge of relevant laws and regulations, including the BMSMA and Strata Titles Act. Good governance and compliance mindset. Good adaptability and communication skills to interact effectively with different groups of stakeholders. Able to work in a fast-paced environment with high volume of transactions. Experienced in property management or facility management in shopping mall or retail environment. Mixed integrated developments, MCST background will be an added advantage. Exhibit strong leadership, people management and interpersonal skills. Ability to lead a team with a positive mindset and self-driven with a desire for knowledge and learning with a can-do attitude. Able to work within tight timelines, resourceful and systematic in resolving problems. Independent and strong interpersonal, analytical, writing and planning skills. Able to handle stress, with ability to prioritize multiple tasks to meet tight deadlines. Be proactive, resourceful, assertive and deliver high quality work. title: Senior Accountant
description: Senior Accountant / Accountant (Retail & E-commerce | Singapore) Our client is a growing retail and e-commerce company with an established presence across physical stores, online platforms, and department store channels. As part of its continued expansion, the company is seeking a hands-on and experienced Senior Accountant / Accountant to oversee the full spectrum of finance operations in Singapore. This is a critical role that will work closely with business stakeholders and play an integral part in financial management, cashflow planning, and business performance. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated finance professional to take ownership of the finance function and contribute to business growth in a dynamic retail and supply chain environment. Key Responsibilities: • Lead and manage the full spectrum of accounting and finance operations for the Singapore business. • Oversee cashflow planning and cash management to ensure smooth operational funding. • Handle GST reporting, IRAS compliance, and corporate tax filings in accordance with local regulations. • Assist in the budgeting and forecasting processes to support strategic and operational planning. • Prepare financial reports, analysis, and insights to support management decision-making. • Manage relationships with banks, including banking facilities, financing arrangements, and all company bank accounts. • Develop, standardize, and strengthen finance policies, processes, and internal controls. • Supervise 1–2 direct reports within the finance team. • Partner with internal stakeholders across retail operations, supply chain, and e-commerce functions. • Support financial matters related to physical retail stores, online sales channels, and department store partnerships. Qualifications & Experience: • Bachelor’s degree in accounting, Finance, or a related field; professional certifications (ACCA/CPA/CA) are an advantage. • Strong experience in full-spectrum accounting and finance operations in a commercial environment. • Solid hands-on knowledge of cashflow management, banking matters, and financial controls. • Confident stakeholder manager, able to work closely with operational and business teams. • Hands-on, proactive, and adaptable, with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.

title: Senior Associate, Treasures Relationship Manager (Offshore - North Asia Market), Consumer Banking Group (WD80882)
topic_label: wealth management services, financial advisory, wealth management
description: DBS Treasures is a leading wealth management platform in Singapore, uniquely positioned to cater to the diverse requirements of both Onshore and Offshore affluent clientele. Our comprehensive offerings provide clients with a seamless and all-encompassing banking experience, complemented by an extensive array of wealth management solutions, spanning from investments to legacy planning. Our Treasures clients have access to personalised advice and support from proficient relationship managers, seasoned investment counsellors, specialised bancassurance professionals and dedicated assistant relationship managers. The DBS Treasures Singapore organisation, which is part of Consumer Banking Group - Singapore, is committed to helping our clients navigate the ever-evolving financial landscape to achieve their individual financial goals and aspirations. As a Treasures Relationship Manager, our clients’ success is ultimately your success. Who you are: You have one goal: creating, growing and strengthening the wealth of our high net-worth customers and their families. This simple yet sophisticated goal drives you to uncover and understand our clients’ every need and deliver the right product solutions to enable their success. You have a strong sense of ownership. You take pride in helping our clients achieve their financial goals at every life stage, and work closely with other specialists within your team to create their success. You’re obsessed about providing a stellar client experience. This means you’re constantly staying abreast of the latest industry trends, investment product & service offerings, and fintech developments. Your role involves: Providing a differentiated suite of financial analysis and advice to uncover investment needs and objectives of our sophisticated clients at different life stages Partner with an experienced team of product specialists and Bancassurance Specialist to offer expert guidance on customised solutions to customers Collaborate with administrative staff to provide clients with professional round-the-clock service for all their banking, borrowing and investment needs. title: Finance Manager
description: About the job Rently is looking for a Finance Manager to own the financial core of the business. We operate a fintech and lending business across Singapore and Dubai. As we scale, the financial complexity of the business is increasing, and we’re investing in stronger planning, visibility, and decision support. We’re looking for someone who deeply understands how money flows through the business and can help leadership make better decisions, faster. This is our first dedicated finance hire and a critical role at Rently. You will sit at the center of planning, performance tracking, and decision support. Your primary focus will be FP&A, financial modelling, unit economics, and cash visibility. You’ll work directly with the CEO and closely with the leadership team. You’ll be supported by internal accountants who handle day-to-day bookkeeping and a fractional CFO who acts as a senior advisor. Your role sits in between: hands-on, analytical, and close to the business. If you like building clarity from messy inputs and want real ownership in an early-stage fintech, this role is for you. What You’ll Do Primary Responsibilities (Day-to-day, must be excellent) 📐 Financial Modelling & Forecasting Build, own and maintain Rently’s core financial model used for planning, fundraising, and decision-making Own rolling forecasts, key assumptions, debt investor reporting and scenario analysis Update projections as the business evolves and flag issues early 📊 Unit Economics & Portfolio Analytics Own unit economics across products and markets Track yields, defaults, provisioning, contribution margins, cohort performance, PAR30, write-offs, and IRR Help leadership understand what is working, what is not, and why 💸 Burn, Runway & Cash Visibility Maintain a clear, up-to-date view of burn, runway, and short-term liquidity Support the CEO on capital deployment decisions and pacing of growth 🤝 Leadership Decision Support Partner with the CEO/Founders and leadership team on pricing, hiring pace, market expansion, and growth vs. profitability trade-offs Prepare clear, concise materials for leadership, board, and investor discussions Secondary Responsibilities (Support / Coordination) 🧾 Accounting & Data Reliability Partner closely with internal and external accountants to ensure clean, timely, and reliable financial data You won’t do day-to-day bookkeeping, but you are accountable for having accurate numbers to drive analysis and planning 📜 Regulatory, Investor & Debt Reporting (Numbers-focused) Support regulatory and compliance reporting in Singapore and the UAE by owning financial inputs (not filings) Support debt investor reporting using existing templates and guidance Provide financial inputs for audits, tax, and licensing processes 🌍 Multi-Market Financial View Consolidate Singapore and Dubai into a single financial view Oversee FX exposure, intercompany considerations, and market-level P&Ls (oversight, not structuring) 📊 Financial Data & Analysis Pull and analyze data directly where possible (without relying heavily on engineering) Build dashboards or analyses to track performance, defaults, and unit economics using available tools Who You Are ✅ FP&A-first, Hands-on Finance Operator ~7–10 years of experience in finance, FP&A, or related roles Strong financial modelling and forecasting skills Comfortable getting into the details and building models yourself ✅ Strong Foundation in Accounting Background in audit or accounting (Big 4 or in-house) strongly preferred You understand how financial statements are built and how to pressure-test numbers ✅ Experience with Capital-Intensive or Lending Models Experience in fintech, lending, BNPL, or other capital-intensive businesses is a strong plus Comfortable working with loan books, portfolios, or cohort-based economics ✅ Clear Thinker & Communicator Able to explain numbers clearly to non-finance teammates Focused on what matters, not just what is technically correct ✅ Independent & Practical Comfortable with ambiguity and imperfect systems Able to create structure, prioritize, and move without heavy direction ⭐ Bonus: Experience at an early-stage or high-growth startup Comfortable pulling data using SQL Experience with BI or analytics tools (Looker, Metabase, Tableau, Power BI) Experience working across multiple markets or entities Tools & System Strong Excel / Google Sheets modelling skills required Experience with accounting systems such as Xero (or similar) preferred Comfortable working with raw data exports and financial systems Growth Path This is a foundational finance hire. As Rently scales, this role has clear upside: Opportunity to grow into a Head of Finance role over time Opportunity to build and manage a small finance team Increasing influence over business strategy, capital planning, and investor relationships Why Join Rently 🏙️ Build a meaningful fintech business – Help shape the future of renting and payments across multiple markets. 🤝 Work closely with leadership – Partner directly with the CEO on the most important decisions in the business. 📈 Real ownership – This is a foundational hire with real responsibility and impact. 🌍 Flexibility & growth – Hybrid setup in Singapore with exposure to regional expansion as we scale. 📢 Note: Direct applicants only — we’re not working with agencies for this role.

Management of commitment agreements and subscription forms including monitoring of commitments, drawdowns, and uncalled commitments. Investors Services Assist with new client onboarding and ensure proper fund and fund structure set ups within the accounting system in accordance to governing documents (i.e., PPM, LPA, etc). Assist senior fund accountants in liaising, discussing, and resolving issues surrounding client onboarding with fund managers and stakeholders. title: Finance Director
description: Shape strategy. Lead finance. Create meaningful impact. We are conducting a confidential executive search for an accomplished and forward-looking Finance Director. Responsibilities span financial planning, budgeting, reporting, funding, and audit activities, in close coordination with internal teams and external stakeholders including government organisations and statutory boards. title: (NEW! ) 2-Hours Career Clarity: A Real-World Exploration Workshop
topic_label: wealth management services, financial advisory, wealth management
description: Location @ Manulife Tower (Nearest MRT: Telok Ayer Station or Raffles Place) 11 February 2026, Wednesday 7pm to 9pm (2 hours) Refreshments Provided Feeling uncertain about your career direction? Whether you’re stepping into your first job or rethinking your next move, most people face the same question: “How do I choose a career I won’t regret later?” This workshop will be a guided, real-world exploration designed to help you gain clarity before making long-term career decisions. Why Join? Instead of sitting and listening, you’ll take part in guided activities that help you reflect, discuss, and discover: ✅ Explore Career Trade-Offs – Understand the real fears and realities of employed roles vs self-employed careers and which risks you’re personally more comfortable managing. ✅ Real-Life Decision Simulation – Step into real-world scenarios people face and experience how career outcomes (including income and responsibility) are created. ✅ Self-Fit & Lifestyle Reflection – Clarify what kind of work, structure, and lifestyle fits you best at this stage of life. ✅ Behind-the-Scenes Industry Insights – Get an honest look at how modern financial advisory careers actually work — beyond stereotypes. ✅ Enjoy a Refreshing Experience – Engage in meaningful discussions while enjoying complimentary refreshments! Who Should Attend? ✅ Fresh graduates exploring career directions ✅ Working professionals considering a career switch ✅ Individuals who want clarity before committing years to a path Important Information: Registration starts 20 minutes before the event! Please arrive on time. Each participant will receive a $40 cash transport token at the end of the workshop. This is a physical event. No zoom session will be conducted. Participants may register for only ONE session. If you have attended this workshop before, we kindly ask you to allow new participants to join. Thank you for your understanding. Requirements: ✅ 21 years old and above ✅ Fresh Graduates, Part-Time Students, Homemakers looking for job flexibility or Mid-Career Switch ✅ Minimum A Levels, Diploma or Degree Holders Seats are limited! Come with curiosity, leave with clarity. Please register below to secure your seat, refreshments only provided for registered attendees.

description: Account Executive Location: Ubi Salary: $3200 - $4200 Working Hours: 5 Days | 9am – 6pm Job Summary Senior Accountant to manage full sets of accounts and lead financial operations for our construction and interior design firm. This role combines accounting rigor with business partnership, project cost control, and automation-driven efficiency. You will take ownership of financial reporting, revenue recognition, and cash flow forecasting while collaborating with cross-functional teams to support profitability and ensure compliance. Key Responsibilities 1. Full Sets of Accounts Maintain complete and accurate financial records, including journal entries, general ledger, and reconciliations. Manage monthly and year-end closing processes, ensuring timely and accurate reporting. 2. Accounts Payable (AP) Oversee invoice processing, vendor management, and timely disbursements. Monitor cash outflows and implement tech-enabled workflows to improve AP efficiency. 3. Accounts Receivable (AR) Generate and track client invoices; manage collections and follow up on overdue payments. Leverage automation to streamline AR tracking and enhance cash collection. 4. Revenue Recognition Ensure accurate and compliant recognition of revenue from project-based services, including progressive billing and milestone-based models. Apply relevant standards such as percentage-of-completion (POC) where applicable. 5. Project Costing & Budget Oversight Track project budgets, WIP (Work-in-Progress) schedules, and actual expenses. Conduct variance analysis and collaborate with project managers to ensure profitability. 6. Financial Reporting & Analysis Prepare and analyze monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements. Provide strategic insights on trends in revenue, cost control, and project performance. 7. Cash Flow & Liquidity Management Monitor daily cash flow and prepare rolling forecasts. Recommend actions to optimize working capital and project funding. 8. Tax & Regulatory Compliance Prepare corporate tax filings and ensure compliance with local tax regulations. Manage GST, withholding tax, and audit-related documentation. 9. Audit & Year-End Preparedness Coordinate with external auditors during the year-end process. Prepare audit schedules and ensure timely, accurate submissions. 10. Cross-Functional Collaboration Partner with design, sales, and project teams to align financial operations with business needs. Translate financial data into actionable insights for decision-making. 11. Policy, Process & Tech Enablement Explore and leverage AI-powered tools (e.g., invoice OCR, predictive analytics, smart reconciliation) to enhance accounting accuracy, forecasting, and operational efficiency. Establish and refine internal controls and accounting procedures. Drive adoption of accounting systems and reporting dashboards to enhance process accuracy and visibility. 12. Continuous Improvement Stay informed on industry best practices, evolving standards, and new tools. Lead or support finance automation and systems improvement initiatives. Requirements : Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field. Minimum 2-3 years of accounting experience, preferably in construction, interior design, architecture, or other project-based environments. Experience in Full Set, AP/AR management and cash flow forecasting, Project Accounting, QuickBooks, Xero and month/year-end processes. Experience with revenue recognition for project-based billing models (POC or milestone-based). Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (e.g., pivot tables, lookups, formulas). Experience with ERP upgrades or system implementations and exposure to Power BI, Google Sheets scripting, or Airtable for reporting automation. title: Global Financial Planning and Analytics Manager
description: The opportunity Hitachi Energy is seeking a Global Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) Manager for the Business Unit Transformers to join our innovative and forward-thinking team. In this role, you will drive financial excellence and strategic decision-making by providing insightful analysis, robust financial planning, and accurate forecasting. You will empower the organization with actionable financial insights, ensuring sustainable growth and profitability while collaborating with cross-functional teams to optimize financial performance. This is your opportunity to grow your leadership skills, contribute to the energy transition, and make a meaningful impact in a dynamic and fast-paced environment. How you will make an impact You will support the strategic direction of regions and GlobalProduct Groups by providing financial insights and conducting research on key industry trends. You will facilitate the preparation and analysis of financial reports for the global business unit organization, including business planning, budgeting, and forecasting. You will enhance business decision-making by crafting insightful profitability analysis for regions, factories, service units, customersegments, and product lines. You will collaborate with finance professionals and function leaders across regions and countries to analyze key financial performance indicators and trends. You will design and prepare advanced financial models to assess various "what if" scenarios and their potential impacts on the business unit. You will lead initiatives to continuously refine financial planning and analysis processes, improving efficiency and accuracy. You will standardize definitions and reporting metrics for productivity and efficiency, driving process improvements that enhance operational efficiency and profitability. You will lead financial audits and ensure compliance with internal control standards, assessing and applying automation to streamline processes across the business unit. Your background Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, ora related field. Minimum of 7 years of experience in financial management, with a proven track record in strategic planning, forecasting, and financial analysis. In-depth knowledge of IFRS, internal controls, audit processes, and risk management practices to ensure compliance and operational integrity. Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills to collaborate effectively across functions and locations. Advanced financial analysis, analytical and critical-thinking capabilities, with proven ability to synthesize and interpret complex financial data to inform strategic decision-making. Strong commitment to continuous improvement by identifying opportunities for process optimization and leveraging digital tools to automate and enhance financial workflows. Ability to inspire and guide teams, fostering a culture of growth and development. At Hitachi Energy, we believe in fostering a collaborative and inclusive work environment. We offer opportunities for growth and development, ensuring our team members can thrive and achieve their career goals. Join us and be part of a company that values innovation, excellence, and teamwork. We pride ourselves on offering a holistic range of competitive benefit programs to support your financial, physical and mental wellbeing and personal development. We want you to truly thrive with us – in work and out. For this role, depending on grade and experience, we offer the following employee benefits. Employer-sponsored medical plan (inc. dental care). Central Provident Fund (CPF). Group Term Life insurance, Group Personal Accident insurance, Group Business Travel insurance. Hospitalization Insurance. description: Bestar is a full-service accounting and corporate services firm in Singapore. A Manager-level role at Bestar involves overseeing a portfolio of clients (ranging from SMEs to international subsidiaries) and leading a team of associates. Job Title: Accounting & Tax Manager Employment Type: Full-Time Job Purpose As an Accounting & Tax Manager at Bestar, you will be responsible for the high-level management of client portfolios, ensuring all financial reporting and taxation requirements are met with precision and in compliance with Singapore’s regulatory frameworks (SFRS and IRAS). Client Portfolio Management Oversee the preparation of full sets of accounts and management reports for a diverse range of clients. Review unaudited financial statements and ensure they comply with Singapore Financial Reporting Standards (SFRS) . Leadership & Operations Manage, mentor, and review the work of Accounts Associates and Senior Associates. Drive workflow efficiency through the adoption of cloud accounting software like Xero or QuickBooks Online . Monitor billing, collections, and the recoverability of team hours for assigned projects. Job Requirements Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Accountancy, Finance, or a related field. Professional Qualification: CA Singapore, ACCA, CPA, or equivalent is highly preferred. SCTP (Singapore Chartered Tax Professionals) accreditation is a significant advantage. Experience: Minimum 6–8 years of relevant experience in a professional accounting firm in Singapore, with at least 2 years in a supervisory or managerial capacity. Technical Skills: * Deep knowledge of Singapore Companies Act and Income Tax Act. Proficiency in cloud accounting software (Xero, QuickBooks) and MS Excel. Soft Skills: Strong leadership, excellent communication (English), and the ability to manage multiple deadlines in a fast-paced environment. Why Join Bestar? title: GC13374551 - Senior Treasury Manager (Shipping MNC)
description: The Company Join a Global Leader in the marine industry, headquartered in Singapore with global presence. Due to evolving business needs, we are actively recruiting for a Senior Treasury Manager to be based in the Singapore office. The Role Reporting to the CFO, you will be responsible for end-to-end liquidity management, financial modelling, and primary bank engagement for the Group. The role integrates treasury execution with forward-looking capital planning to ensure strong cash visibility, disciplined funding decisions, and a single, credible financial narrative with lenders and stakeholders. This role sits at the intersection of cash management, forecasting, modelling, and banker communication, supporting the CFO on funding strategy, refinancing, and covenant management. The key responsibilities include daily and medium-term liquidity management across the Group. Develop and maintain integrated financial models (cash flow, funding, covenant, stress scenarios). Support refinancing, restructuring, and funding proposals through robust modelling. Ensure modelling assumptions are aligned with actual cash behaviour and treasury realities. Act as the primary point of contact for relationship banks and lenders. Prepare and present cash flow forecasts, funding updates, and covenant reporting to banks. Support negotiations on facilities, terms, waivers, and renewals in collaboration with the CFO. Coordinate bank information requests, ensuring consistency and credibility of data shared. Monitor compliance with financial covenants and reporting obligations. Prepare covenant calculations, headroom analysis, and early-warning indicators. Proactively flag risks and recommend mitigating actions to the CFO. Strengthen treasury governance, controls, and reporting discipline. Drive automation and process improvements (e.g. ERP / Treasury systems) to reduce manual effort. Ensure clear audit trails and documentation for treasury and funding decisions. Partner with Finance, FP&A, and Accounting teams to align forecasts and assumptions. Support the CFO with materials for Board, lenders, and internal steering committees. Contribute to capital structure strategy and long-term funding planning discussions. Your Profile To qualify for this role, you must have a Bachelor Degree in Accountancy with minimum 10 years of relevant experience in a MNC with experience in treasury, corporate finance and capital planning. This role requires hands-on experience in financial modelling with practical cash-flow orientation. We are looking for a meticulous, analytical, technically competent Treasury professional with solid understanding of debt instruments, covenants and liquidity management. title: Senior Finance Manager

You will help ensure the company scales quickly without financial fragility. If you are looking for a passive reporting role, this is not it. If you want to build systems that will hold up through growth, fundraising, and scrutiny, this role is for you. We’re looking for a Finance Lead who can own the numbers end-to-end - from day-to-day accounting to audits, board reporting, and scaling our financial systems for the next stage of growth. This is not a back-office role. You will sit close to the CEO, investors, auditors, and operators - and help the company move faster because finance is rock solid. Responsibilities Accounting & Financial Operations Own full-set accounting: GL, AP/AR, accruals, revenue recognition, and cash management Lead monthly, quarterly, and annual close with accuracy and speed Maintain audit-ready financials at all times Audit & Compliance Act as primary owner of statutory audits Liaise directly with external auditors, tax advisors, and regulators Ensure compliance with Singapore accounting and statutory requirements (SFRS/IFRS) Prepare statutory financial statements and supporting schedules Financial Planning & Control Build and maintain budgets, forecasts, and cash-flow models Track burn rate, runway, and cost structure Establish financial controls and internal processes appropriate for a scaling company Leadership & Stakeholder Support Work closely with the CEO and leadership team Support investor reporting, board materials, and due diligence Assist with fundraising, grants, and corporate structuring as needed Systems & Scale Design and improve accounting systems, tooling, and workflows Prepare the company to scale from early-stage to institutional standards Qualifications 5–10 years of experience in accounting, finance, or audit Strong experience owning full-set accounts Proven experience managing audits end-to-end Experience in startups, high-growth tech companies, or fast-moving environments preferred Solid understanding of Singapore accounting standards and compliance High attention to detail with strong execution discipline Preferred but not required CA / CPA / ACCA or equivalent qualification Experience with VC-backed companies or multi-entity structures What We Look For High ownership and accountability Comfortable operating in ambiguity and building structure from scratch Strong judgment under pressure Ability to balance speed with correctness Direct, clear, and precise communicator Why This Role Matters Finance is a core system, not a support function. Reporting to: Finance Manager and Head of Finance Requirements 2–3 years of relevant experience (non-profit, startup, or SME experience preferred). Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (or equivalent experience). Proficiency in Xero and familiarity with the Airwallex ecosystem. The ideal candidate brings deep technical accounting expertise, experience in listed companies or listing environments, strong business and operational acumen, and a proven track record in people leadership and mergers & acquisitions. Key Responsibilities Strategic & Business Leadership Provide financial leadership and strategic guidance across the organization, influencing business performance, capital planning, and risk management. Partner with senior management to support corporate strategy, growth initiatives, and operational decision-making through data-driven financial insights. End-to-End Finance & Accounting Operations Oversee the full spectrum of finance and accounting functions including GL, AP/AR, payroll, taxation, treasury, financial reporting, and compliance. Ensure timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reporting in accordance with statutory and regulatory requirements. Lead audits, tax matters, and governance processes with a high standard of integrity and control. Listing & Regulatory Compliance Manage financial governance and compliance requirements for a listed company or companies preparing for listing. Ensure adherence to relevant accounting standards, regulatory frameworks, and reporting obligations. Budgeting, Forecasting & Financial Planning Lead budgeting, forecasting, and long-term financial planning processes. Provide insightful analysis on performance, variances, and profitability to support executive decision-making. Mergers & Acquisitions Lead end-to-end M&A activities including financial due diligence, valuation, deal structuring, integration planning, and post-merger financial integration. Partner with internal and external stakeholders to ensure successful execution of transactions. Risk Management & Internal Controls Establish and maintain robust internal controls, financial policies, and risk management frameworks. Proactively identify financial, operational, and compliance risks and implement mitigation strategies. People Leadership & Team Development Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing finance team. Build a strong finance organization with clear accountability, capability development, and succession planning. Foster strong collaboration with cross-functional leaders across the business. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field. Professional accounting certification is mandatory (CPA, ACCA, CA, or equivalent). Experience working in a listed company or with listing exposure is required. Proven people management experience leading finance teams. Demonstrated experience managing end-to-end finance and accounting functions. Hands-on experience with mergers & acquisitions. Strong business, operational, and commercial acumen. Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and leadership skills.

title: Senior Finance Manager / Deputy Finance Controller
description: Role Overview The Senior Finance Manager / Deputy Finance Controller is a senior finance role responsible for overseeing end-to-end finance functions while supporting the CFO or Finance Controller in strategic, financing, and governance matters. The role covers full-spectrum financial management including accounting, reporting, budgeting, business finance, and capital management, with selected people management responsibilities. While the Group is not currently listed, the role requires experience in listed-company environments and a solid understanding of capital markets, governance, and reporting standards to support future readiness. Key Responsibilities 1. Financial Management & Reporting (Full-Scope) •	Oversee core finance functions including financial accounting, management reporting, budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis. •	Ensure timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reporting in compliance with applicable accounting standards and internal policies. •	Work closely with the Finance Controller on statutory reporting, audits, and financial close processes. 2. Business Finance & Performance Management •	Partner with business units and senior management to provide financial analysis, performance insights, and decision support. •	Support business planning, cost control, capital expenditure evaluation, and profitability analysis. •	Provide forward-looking analysis and scenario planning to support management decision-making. 3. Capital, Financing & Treasury Support •	Support and execute financing activities including bank facilities, refinancing, and funding arrangements. •	Prepare cash flow forecasts, funding analyses, and financial models to support liquidity and capital planning. •	Monitor banking facilities, covenant compliance, and treasury-related matters. •	Maintain working relationships with banks, financial institutions, and external advisors. 4. Financial Governance, Compliance & Listed-Company Readiness •	Ensure strong financial governance, internal controls, and compliance with regulatory and group requirements. •	Support audit processes, including coordination with external auditors and advisors. •	Apply listed-company standards and best practices in financial reporting, disclosures, internal controls, and governance, where relevant. •	Support preparation of board and senior management materials with appropriate governance standards. 5. Cross-Functional & Stakeholder Engagement •	Work closely with the CFO, Finance Controller, and internal teams to support finance operations and strategic initiatives. •	Support communication and relationship management with key external stakeholders, including but not limited to banks, shareholders, auditors, and government-related agencies, on finance-related matters. •	Coordinate and prepare financial information, analysis, and materials for stakeholder discussions and engagements. •	Contribute to continuous improvement of finance processes, controls, and reporting standards. •	Provide ad-hoc financial support and analysis for management requests and special projects as required. Key Requirements Professional Background •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline from a reputable local university is strongly preferred; a Master’s degree is an advantage. •	Professional qualification such as CPA, CA, ACCA, or equivalent is preferred. •	Prior experience in Big 4 accounting firms in Singapore is a strong advantage. •	Experience in listed companies is required, with exposure to financial reporting, governance, and capital market-related practices. •	Experience in China or PRC-related business environments is an advantage. Experience & Skills •	Strong hands-on experience across full finance functions, including accounting, reporting, budgeting, and business finance. •	Working knowledge of financing, treasury, and banking arrangements. •	Solid understanding of Singapore’s regulatory framework, corporate governance, and listed-company standards. •	Strong analytical, financial modelling, and presentation skills. •	Able to communicate effectively with senior management, auditors, banks, and external advisors. Personal Attributes •	Strong sense of ownership with a practical, hands-on approach. •	Able to balance operational detail with strategic perspective. •	High level of professionalism, discretion, and accountability. •	Comfortable working independently while supporting senior finance leadership. •	Fluent in English; proficiency in Mandarin is an advantage.

description: GIC is one of the world’s largest sovereign wealth funds. With over 2,000 employees across 11 locations around the world, we invest in more than 40 countries globally across asset classes and businesses. Working at GIC gives you exposure to an extraordinary network of the world’s industry leaders. As a leading global long-term investor, we Work at the Point of Impact for Singapore’s financial future, and the communities we invest in worldwide. Data Strategy Group Data Strategy Group (DSG) harnesses and leverages on GIC’s most valuable asset – Data, to drive insights, results and solve real business challenges. We ingest, analyse, and combine data with context across all industries and asset classes to enable investment decisions and enhance investment process. Together, we strive to build a high-performance enterprise data infrastructure, create results-driven approach with data products and strive to be a data-driven organization. Data Engineering You will help to engineer outstanding data solutions to deliver data services at scale. Using modern and innovative data technologies, the team enables GIC colleagues to securely, and seamlessly access data. What impact can you make in this role? You will be empowered to be at the top of your game by providing technical contributions to achieve data-driven outcomes to power critical investment data & analytics in GIC’s vision of being a tech-driven, global, long-term investment firm. What will you do as a Data Engineer? Be part of an ambitious team that provides investment data services for enterprise activities. You can make a difference in our forward-looking journey to shape our world of investments. Work in engineering teams to build and support enterprise investment data & analytics systems consumed by investment professionals and platforms across the firm. Develop business solutions using your strong analytical skills, and contribute to the team in delivering technology value directly to the business. Perform regular peer code reviews and provide technical coaching to junior team members, and sharing to raise the quality bar for engineering talent. Be actively involved in software delivery for fast-moving agile projects with modern DevSecOps practices (e.g. Github, Jfrog, Jira) and data technologies (eg. Python, Snowflake/Databricks, DBT, Airflow). Push for greater automation and automated testing to improve productivity and reduce operational risks. Become an excellent team player with a collaborative mind-set, you will work directly with investment and technology professionals at different levels. What makes you a successful candidate? Join us if you have a passion for data and want to be part of a team in building and developing GIC’s data foundation and infrastructure. 4 or more years of relevant technology experience preferably in financial risk services or asset management industries, and experience in leading software development teams Good engineering experience & practices in the following:  SQL, and/or relational database – Postgres, Oracle, Snowflake Python/Pandas/DBT/PySpark Java / C# (.NET Core), and/or similar OOP frameworks Unix, Shell scripting (Optional) Docker / Kubernetes / Github workflow, and/or other devops capabilities  (Optional) AWS services such as Glue/Batch, S3, SQS, SNS, EKS (Optional) Federated query tools such as Denodo, Presto/Trino, Starburst Willingness to learn with attention to detail, demonstrating a strong passion in technology and/or finance Fair presentation skills, and communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences Good engagement with software engineers and guide hands-on development Ability to collaborate with cross-departmental business and technical partners to contribute in the delivery of business outcomes Bachelor’s or equivalent degree in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) related discipline is advantageous Work at the Point of Impact We need to be forward-looking to attract the right people to help us become the Leading Global Long-term Investor. Join our ambitious, agile, and diverse teams - be empowered to push boundaries and pursue innovative ideas, share your views, and be heard. Be anchored on our PRIME Values: Prudence, Respect, Integrity, Merit and Excellence, which guides us in how we make our day-to-day decisions. We strive to inspire. To make an impact. Flexibility at GIC At GIC, our offices are vibrant hubs for ideation, professional growth, and interpersonal connection.  At the same time, we believe that flexibility allows us to do our best work and be our best selves.  Thus, our teams come into the office four days per week to harness the benefits of in-person collaboration, but have the flexibility to choose which days they work from home and adjust this arrangement as situational needs arise.
