description: Key Responsibilities Database Migration Support •                  Support end-to-end database migration activities, including data validation, transformation, and reconciliation. •                  Assist in migrating data sources that underpin large-scale visualization dashboards. •                  Identify and resolve data integrity, performance, and compatibility issues arising from migration. Data Engineering & Platform Management •                  Develop and maintain SQL and Python-based data pipelines using Databricks and Jupyter Notebooks. •                  Create and manage Databricks compute clusters and SQL Warehouses; and configure Unity Catalog for data governance and access control. •                  Optimize queries and data models to ensure performance and scalability post-migration. BI & Dashboard Enablement •                  Reconfigure, test, and validate Tableau dashboards impacted by database changes. •                  Partner with dashboard owners to ensure data accuracy, performance, and functional parity after migration. •                  Support ad-hoc analysis and troubleshooting related to Tableau and SpotFire dashboards. Collaboration & Documentation •                  Work closely with Data Analysts, Business Stakeholders, and IT teams within ASM. •                  Document migration steps, data mappings, and known issues to support knowledge transfer and audit readiness. •                  Follow ASM data governance, security, and compliance standards. •                  Collaborate with global network/database team to identify, engineer and key data elements in-line with existing needs. Required Skills & Qualifications Technical Skills (Mandatory) •                  Tableau certification (required; active or recently obtained). •                  Strong proficiency in SQL / MySQL for querying, transformation, and validation. •                  Hands-on experience with Python for data processing and automation. Experience using Databricks, including: Creating and managing compute clusters and SQL Warehouses. Working with Unity Catalog •                  Experience working with Jupyter Notebooks. Technical Skills (Nice-to-Have / Highly Favored) •                  Experience with SpotFire (dashboard development or support). •                  Prior involvement in database or platform migration projects. •                  Familiarity with manufacturing, biotech, or regulated environments.

title: WTT Finance Director
description: ABOUT WORLD TABLE TENNIS World Table Tennis is where the sport’s fiercest battles meet bold ideas and creative minds. WTT is powered by a global crew of innovators, storytellers, and game-changers, who bring energy, expertise, and imagination to everything they do. We’re not your typical workplace; we’re the global engine behind a sport on the rise. So whether it’s staging iconic global events or redefining fan experiences, this is where bold ideas get built, and where every role has impact. At WTT, the arena simply hits different. FINANCE DIRECTOR To lead the corporate group’s finance function end-to-end, ensuring strong financial governance, reliable reporting, disciplined cashflow management, and commercial finance partnership to support sustainable growth. This role owns the “finance operating system”; standards, controls, risk posture, stakeholder reporting, and decision support while developing a high-performing lean team. RESPONSIBILITIES FINANCE LEADERSHIP & EXECUTIVE PARTNERING Act as a trusted advisor to CEO/senior leadership/Board/shareholders on group financial performance, governance, and strategic decisions. Translate financial data into clear options and recommendations (trade-offs, risks, scenario impacts). Build a finance culture that is commercially minded, control-conscious, and operationally pragmatic. FINANCIAL REPORTING GOVERNANCE & CLOSE OVERSIGHT Own the finance calendar, reporting cadence, and quality gates (e.g., close timeline, reconciliations standards, review checklist). Review and sign off management accounts, forecasts, and key reporting before distribution to CEO/Board/shareholders. Ensure accounting treatments are appropriate, consistent, and audit-ready (including revenue recognition implications relevant to events/rights-based revenue). TREASURY, CASH STRATEGY & FINANCIAL RISK CONTROL Own group-level cash strategy: runway planning, liquidity thresholds, and cash visibility. Maintain and review a rolling cash forecast discipline (e.g., weekly 13-week view where needed). Oversee banking relationships, payment controls, signatory governance, and (where relevant) FX exposure management. Partner with HR on payroll planning and execution oversight (timeliness, accuracy, approvals). FINANCIAL CONTROLS, GOVERNANCE & COMPLIANCE Design, implement, and enforce the group’s Delegation of Authority (DoA) and financial control framework (approval flows, spend controls, credit controls). Monitor compliance, investigate control breaches, and drive remediation — keeping the business moving without letting it get sloppy. Own finance policies/SOPs and ensure the team operates consistently across entities. FP&A LEADERSHIP (Budgeting, Forecasting, Performance insights) Lead annual budget cycle and reforecast cadence; set standards for assumptions, documentation, and sign-offs. Deliver insight-led performance commentary (variance drivers, scenario planning, sensitivities). Build KPI reporting aligned to the business model (event profitability, margin drivers, cash conversion, pipeline impact). COMMERCIAL FINANCE & DECISION SUPPORT Partner business leads on pricing/margins, contract payment terms, cost models, and profitability analysis across events/sponsorship/broadcast/ticketing. Ensure strategic and/or long-term growth projects (e.g. strategic partnerships, joint ventures, product launches and redevelopment, investments and market entries) have finance-reviewed business cases with measurable outcomes. Provide post-event financial performance views and lessons learned to improve future planning. AUDIT, TAX & SHAREHOLDER REPORTING LEADERSHIP Own auditor relationship, audit plan/timeline, and closure of audit findings. Ensure tax governance: timely filings, appropriate positions, and oversight of complex tax matters (GST/VAT, withholding tax, income tax as applicable). Partner shareholder finance teams on consolidated reporting requirements and governance expectations. TEAM LEADERSHIP, OPERATING MODEL & CAPABILITY BUILDING Lead, coach, and develop the finance team; set clear standards, delegation, and accountability. Ensure finance work is scalable: documented processes, cross-training, and sensible automation. Drive continuous improvement of tools and workflows (accounting, approvals, payments) while ensuring controls are robust and processes remain efficient and user-friendly. REQUIREMENTS Essentials: 10+ years relevant finance experience. Professional qualification: CA / CPA / ACCA (or equivalent). Proven people management experience (leading and developing finance staff & building operating rhythm and standards). Strong competency across reporting, controls, FP&A, audit, and tax governance. High proficiency in MS365 especially Excel and comfort working with modern finance tools. Desirables: Experience in startups/high-growth companies (e.g., scaling finance operations; exposure to Series funding rounds). Experience working with diverse stakeholder groups across cultures and seniority (including shareholders and auditors). Industry exposure to sport, entertainment, media rights-based or event-led businesses.Familiarity with finance and payments systems such as Xero, Syft, ApprovalMax, Zoho, Airwallex (or similar). HOW TO APPLY Please proceed to the World Table Tennis page - careers.worldtabletennis.com Due to the high number of candidate applications expected, only those who are shortlisted will be contacted.

title: Fresh Graduates - Bank Operations
description: This is a back-office operations role in a bank’s Card Operations Processing Centre, specifically within the Reconciliation and Investigation Unit. Your main responsibility is to ensure that the bank’s financial records match actual money movement, particularly for credit card transactions. Responsibilities: - Daily reconciliation/proofing of General Ledger (GL) and Nostro (Cash accounts) on transactions for Cards Operations Processing Centre - Escalation of outstanding items for the day, and age items for both Nostro and General Ledger (GL) - Invoice reconciliation" (A) Key Duties Explained: - Daily Reconciliation of Accounts: You will compare and match bank records (General Ledger) with actual cash balances (Nostro accounts) to ensure there are no discrepancies. Think of this as daily “balancing the books” — making sure the bank’s internal system reflects the real-world financial activity. - Escalating Outstanding Items: If any mismatch or error is found (e.g., a payment didn’t go through or an amount doesn’t match), you will flag or escalate it to the relevant team for investigation and resolution. - Aging of Items: You'll monitor unresolved discrepancies over time. “Aging” refers to tracking how long items remain unmatched and ensuring they are not left unresolved for too long. - Invoice Reconciliation: You will help match invoices received (e.g., from vendors or third-party processors) against the bank’s records, ensuring correctness before payment or settlement. (B) Skills You’ll Use: - Attention to detail (to spot mismatches), Familiarity with Excel or reconciliation tools, Ability to follow up and escalate when issues arise, Time management (to complete daily checks promptly) - Who is This Job Suitable For: Fresh graduates in banking, finance, or business, or Candidates with 1–2 years of back-office banking experience, Someone who prefers structured, process-oriented work rather than customer-facing roles

description: We are seeking a Technical Support Engineer for our client in the chemical manufacturing industry to be responsible for providing technical expertise and application support to drive the growth of the engineering plastics business regionally. Plan, conduct, and evaluate analysis methods based on actual application requirements to generate relevant technical data for customer development projects. Conduct molding trials with customers to validate material and application performance. Investigate mechanical and physical properties of engineering plastics using scientific instruments to support new product and application development. Perform failure benchmarking, root cause analysis, and destructive or non-destructive testing to identify material or application defects. Translate customer application requirements into material property specifications and collaborate with R&D to develop or formulate new materials. Support technical discussions and negotiations with customers on performance limitations of newly developed materials. Work closely with the commercial team to develop mid-term and long-term business strategies for target applications and markets. Provide technical support to the commercial team to achieve short-term sales and budget targets. Conduct market research on emerging technologies and industry trends relevant to current and future applications. Support technical marketing initiatives to develop new markets and customers. Provide technical training to distributors, traders, and business partners; extend training to customers when required. Maintain accurate technical documentation, test reports, experimental records, and procedures. Support and participate in trade shows, exhibitions, and technical seminars to enhance product branding and market presence. Requirements: Degree in Polymer Science, Materials Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related discipline. Experience in engineering plastics, polymers, or related materials industries is a must Strong knowledge of material testing, failure analysis, and injection molding processes. Ability to translate application needs into technical and material solutions. Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross-functionally with commercial, R&D, and customer teams. title: Data Engineer(Hadoop and Pyspark)_Contract
topic_label: data engineer, data engineers, data analyst
description: We’re looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to design, build, and optimize scalable data pipelines on Hadoop ecosystem leveraging PySpark. You will partner with data scientists, analytics teams, and product engineering to deliver reliable datasets and streaming/Batch pipelines that power business insights and ML use cases. Key Responsibilities Design & build data pipelines using PySpark/Spark (Batch & Streaming), integrating data from diverse sources (RDBMS, APIs, files, Kafka). Develop and optimize ETL/ELT workflows on Hadoop ecosystem (HDFS, Hive, Spark), ensuring data quality, lineage, and reliability. Model data for analytics/BI/ML (star/snowflake schemas, partitioning, bucketing) and implement efficient storage formats (Parquet/ORC). Orchestrate workflows using Airflow (or similar schedulers) with robust dependency management, retries, alerting, and SLA monitoring. Implement streaming pipelines with Kafka (or similar), including windowed aggregations, exactly-once semantics (where applicable), and schema evolution management. Enable data governance & security (RBAC, masking, encryption at-rest/in-transit, audit logging, schema registry). Performance tuning & cost optimization across Spark configs, shuffle strategies, broadcast joins, caching, and resource sizing. Automate CI/CD for data pipelines (unit/integration tests, data quality checks, deployment automation, infrastructure-as-code). Collaborate cross-functionally with data scientists, analytics, and platform teams to define SLAs, data contracts, and consumption patterns. Documentation & support : Maintain runbooks, metadata, lineage, and provide L2/L3 support for production incidents. Required Qualifications 2–6 years of experience in data engineering (adjust as needed), with strong expertise in PySpark and the Hadoop ecosystem (HDFS, Hive, Spark). Advanced Python and SQL skills (analytical functions, performance tuning). Experience with workflow orchestration (Airflow/Luigi/Prefect) and version control (Git). Hands-on with data warehousing and modeling concepts; experience optimizing large-scale distributed computations. Exposure to streaming (Kafka, Spark Structured Streaming) and schema management . Experience in at least one cloud platform (AWS EMR/Glue, Azure HDInsight/Synapse/Databricks, or GCP Dataproc/BigQuery). Strong understanding of data quality (DQ rules, Great Expectations or equivalent), metadata , and lineage . Familiarity with Linux , shell scripting, and containerization ( Docker ); basic understanding of CI/CD . Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and problem‑solving skills.

Job Description The Process
Engineer is responsible for developing, sustaining, and improving semiconductor
and advanced materials manufacturing processes to meet customer and business

     recipes. Perform process
     characterization, qualification, and validation activities to ensure

     and guidance to technicians and operators to ensure compliance with
     process requirements and quality standards. Lead or participate in cost
     reduction, yield improvement, and quality improvement projects. Collaborate with internal
     teams and customers on process, material, and equipment capability
     development and improvement. Support audits, customer
     technical reviews, and production ramp-up activities. Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree in
     Engineering (Materials, Mechanical, Chemical, Physics, or related field). Relevant experience in
     semiconductor, advanced materials, or precision manufacturing
     environments. Knowledge of process
     control methodologies such as SPC, root cause analysis, and process
     qualification. Experience with thin-film
     deposition (PVD), plating, or related advanced manufacturing processes is
     preferred. Ability to work
     cross-functionally with production, quality, and engineering teams. Japanese-speaking
     capability is an added advantage for coordination
     with Japanese customers and suppliers.
